name: Budget Insights
description: RECOMMENDED APPROACH A — ON-DEMAND IMMUTABLE INSIGHT REPORT COMPILER. On one explicit structured invocation, INSIGHTS consumes one version-compatible BUDGET-owned composite evidence read that binds the immutable plan, exact Budget Position, and bounded dated members from one public LEDGER snapshot. It computes deterministic exact metrics and Linear Pace results plus explicit states for any separately activated Forecast, Trend, Anomaly, and Attention policy; every emitted result cites its Analysis Window, As-of Instant, formula/version, source snapshots, evidence sufficiency, and limitations. Unsupported or sparse evidence yields an explicit unavailable or insufficient-evidence state, never invented confidence. The successful result may be retained as an immutable owner-only Report Snapshot; later corrections create a separately cited Restatement rather than rewriting what was shown. INSIGHTS mutates no LEDGER, CLASSIFY, or BUDGET state, runs in the existing self-contained CLI process, starts no daemon, and grants CHANNELS no semantics beyond the released read contract. Complexity M, fits the initial budget, root-problem completeness 9/10, and builds directly on FR-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-PROJECTION, DD-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-READ-PROJECTION, and the existing hard LEDGER/BUDGET dependencies. Pros: explainable, reproducible, local, correction-aware, and channel-neutral. Cons: formula and evidence policies must be explicit, and deterministic v1 signals may be less adaptive than richer models. Biggest risk: sparse or irregular spending makes mathematically valid outputs look more predictive than they are. Stress test: representative owner-only evidence must prove that at least one report shape adds timely, correctly caveated value beyond raw Budget Position inspection; otherwise use the do-less path.

ALTERNATIVE B — CONTINUOUS MATERIALIZED ANALYTICS LEDGER. Replicate transaction, category, plan, and correction events into a separate analytical store, continuously update projections, and schedule report generation. Complexity H, outside the initial M budget, root-problem completeness 10/10, and only partly builds on the explicit-invocation architecture. Pros: fast longitudinal queries, rich time series, and natural scheduled delivery. Cons: duplicates authoritative financial state, introduces synchronization and replay correctness, requires a daemon/scheduler and operational recovery, and couples INSIGHTS prematurely to CHANNELS. Biggest risk: the analytical copy silently drifts from corrected LEDGER/BUDGET truth while producing confident reports. Rejected for v1; reconsider only through a project ADR if measured on-demand performance or a future channel requires it.

ALTERNATIVE C — DO LESS WITH BUDGET OUTPUTS PLUS AN EXTERNAL AGENT OR SPREADSHEET. Publish no INSIGHTS state or formulas; let the owner or AI Agent Host interpret Budget Position results case by case and retain any notes externally. Complexity L, fits the budget, root-problem completeness 4/10, and maximally reuses BUDGET. Pros: no new persistence or analytical semantics and immediate manual flexibility. Cons: formulas, windows, corrections, evidence limits, and retained comparisons vary per invocation; private data may spread into uncontrolled artifacts. Biggest risk: plausible but inconsistent advice becomes less trustworthy than the raw position. Retained as the monitored fallback if a deterministic module does not demonstrate additional value.

SELECTION AND BOUNDARIES. Choose Approach A because it earns durable interpretation without duplicating financial truth or introducing an always-on service. The non-obvious inversion is to treat the report as an immutable evidence artifact, not a dashboard: CHANNELS can later render or deliver it, while INSIGHTS owns only analytical semantics and provenance. LEDGER remains authoritative for canonical transactions, relationships, categories, corrected actual membership, and snapshot identity. CLASSIFY improves category coverage but is not required; Uncategorized Spend stays explicit. BUDGET remains authoritative for Budget Plans, Budget Plan Revisions, exact Budget Positions, and plan mutation. INSIGHTS owns formula versions, evidence sufficiency, Insight Reports, Report Snapshots, Restatements, and attention semantics. CHANNELS owns screens, conversation, scheduling requests, and delivery.

SCOPE SIGNALS AND ROUTING. Signals present: high-impact security/privacy for sensitive financial reports; hard external integrations with BUDGET and LEDGER plus a soft CLASSIFY dependency; a likely analytical model of Report Snapshot, signal, metric, threshold, provenance, and restatement entities; cross-module snapshot flows; and significant unknowns around time windows, forecast validity, evidence sufficiency, correction/restatement semantics, and useful attention thresholds. Five signals are present, with security and significant unknowns independently high-impact. Route to /lex:discovery INSIGHTS before PRD. No new project ADR is required for the selected in-process read-only shape; any future materialized analytical replica, background scheduler, network service, cloud/LLM processing, or mutation authority requires reassessment and likely /lex:adr.

DISCOVERY 0.2 — ACTORS AND WORKFLOWS. Human actor: reuse project persona P1, Personal Finance Owner, the sole decision-maker and host-account owner. System actors and boundaries: AI Agent Host discovers and invokes structured operations; BUDGET supplies immutable plan revisions and exact Budget Positions; LEDGER supplies canonical corrected dated actual evidence; Host OS Security protects local process, files, temporary artifacts, and recovery targets; future CHANNELS consumes released report semantics but owns presentation and delivery. CLASSIFY is not invoked by INSIGHTS: its soft contribution arrives through current LEDGER category allocation, and incomplete classification remains explicit.

Current workflow: P1 reads statement history and exact Budget Positions, manually chooses daily/weekly/monthly windows, repeats calculations or asks an external agent/spreadsheet, decides whether sparse evidence is meaningful, and keeps any conclusions outside Tally. Window definitions, formulas, corrections, evidence limits, and what was previously shown are not reproducible. Desired workflow: P1 or the Agent Host discovers one versioned operation, explicitly selects a Budget Period, receives one deterministic report with the trusted operation-start As-of Instant over mutually coherent public evidence, inspects each value/state and its provenance, explicitly retains selected reports, explicitly requests correction-based Restatements with differences, and performs any financial action outside INSIGHTS. UC-INSIGHTS-001 through UC-INSIGHTS-006 cover generation, explanation, retention, restatement, history/recovery, and structured invocation.

PRE-MORTEM. Failure 1: sparse, refund-heavy, front-loaded, or irregular evidence produces mathematically valid but confidently misleading Forecasts or high-severity Attention Items. Early signs are short horizons, unstable baselines, many unavailable comparisons, and results that change sharply after one transaction. Control: per-signal Evidence Sufficiency, exposed limitations, private time-separated counterexamples, zero false high-severity acceptance, and remove unsupported signals. Failure 2: a Budget Position and separately queried dated actuals bind different corrected LEDGER states. Early signs are different snapshot identities, an intervening correction, totals that cannot reconcile, or no common state-version token. Control: C15 and A3 require a bounded authoritative producer seam and concurrent-correction proof before design; no private read or duplicated calculation fallback. Failure 3: retained reports concentrate sensitive behavior and leak through process arguments, diagnostics, exports, backups, crash artifacts, or shared temp paths. Early signs are permissive modes, payload-bearing errors, private paths, or seeded canaries outside the requested business result. Control: owner-only artifacts, minimal retained source data, canary scans across all failure/recovery flows, and the existing persistence kill criterion. Failure 4: too many Attention Items become ignored. Early signs are repeated medium/high items, unstable tie ordering, or no named owner decision improved. Control: hard count cap, built-in thresholds, deterministic ranking, CHANNELS-owned delivery, and the do-less comparison.

DOMAIN DISPOSITIONS. Authentication/authorization — CHECK: v1 reuses the sole host-account boundary and P1 attribution; no app users, roles, tenants, tokens, or network identity. A second user, remote client, or service writer invalidates this disposition. Data model/persistence — DEEP: optional immutable Report Snapshots, request identity, Restatement links, deletion dependencies, fingerprints, migration, backup, and separate-target restore are governed by C8/C14/C16/C17 and UC-INSIGHTS-003/004/005. UI/navigation — N/A: CHANNELS owns every human-facing screen, conversation, accessibility, translation, and delivery surface. Integrations — DEEP: BUDGET/LEDGER public reads, their cross-read coherence gate, Agent Host invocation, Host OS, and future CHANNELS are explicit external dependencies. Background/async — N/A: generation and restatement are explicit invocation only; no watcher, daemon, scheduler, queue, automatic correction scan, or unsolicited delivery. Reporting/analytics/time — DEEP: explicit windows, exact observed metrics, formula-specific sufficiency, comparable identities, signals, limitations, attention ordering, and unavailable states are example-mapped; OQ-INSIGHTS-2/4/5/6 remain bounded PRD or spike decisions. Notifications — N/A: INSIGHTS emits Attention Items but never delivery policy or notification state. Configuration/admin — CHECK: v1 ships a bounded catalogue of released policy versions; NG11/C18 exclude owner-authored formulas, scripts, queries, and plugins. Multi-tenancy/isolation — N/A: one trusted local P1 and ZAR only; cross-household or multi-user scope triggers re-discovery. Security/privacy/compliance — DEEP: financial payload minimization, permissions, process boundaries, compatibility, canary tests, recovery, and no-network rules apply. No additional product compliance workflow is identified inside the accepted personal/household local scope; hosted, shared, professional, cross-border, or third-party processing requires a fresh compliance assessment.

INTEGRATED ANALYTICS RULES AND EXAMPLES. Exact metrics and derived signals are independent: exact plan/actual/remaining evidence may be available while Forecast, Trend, Anomaly, or Attention Items are unavailable. Pace succeeds only with a valid plan, elapsed explicit window, complete coherent actuals, and defined exact arithmetic; absent plan, zero-length interval, snapshot mismatch, overflow, or undefined division returns a named state. Forecast succeeds only for a supported evidence shape meeting its formula-specific horizon and coverage; sparse, irregular, correction-dominated, refund-only, or otherwise unsupported shapes emit insufficient evidence. Trend or Anomaly requires stable category identity and comparable explicit windows/policy versions; rename alone does not change identity, while replacement, lifecycle discontinuity, incompatible coverage, or changed policy can make only the affected comparison unavailable. Attention Items require one cited supported metric/signal, explicit threshold/severity, stable tie-breakers, and hard cap; unavailable, stale, or insufficient evidence never creates one. Restatement is an explicit request that preserves the predecessor; an exactly unchanged result returns No Change, while a changed result creates one linked report only after complete success and returns differences separated by source, policy, exact value, state, and attention changes.

INTEGRATION SPIKE RESULT. The prior BUDGET capability exposed only budget.plan.revision.get and budget.position.get, while a separate LEDGER actuals call could observe a later correction. The 2026-07-20 A3 spike forced that race 1000 times and mixed generations every time. A BUDGET-owned budget.insights.evidence.get composite that reused one materialized public LEDGER snapshot for both the canonical BudgetPositionCalculator and returned dated members produced 1000 coherent pre-correction and 1000 coherent post-correction results, with zero mixed generations and zero reconciliation failures. DD-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE governs the chosen shape. A3 is validated; production conformance remains required. Private storage access, replicated truth, or reimplementation of BUDGET position logic still triggers the first kill criterion.

SECURITY AND TRUST CHECK. P1/Agent Host to INSIGHTS input is checked for spoofed attribution, tampering, disclosure, and resource exhaustion; financial input uses stdin or an approved owner-only file. INSIGHTS to BUDGET/LEDGER is checked for incompatible schemas, authority expansion, mixed snapshots, incomplete pagination, and stale generations before output or persistence. Retained store/backup/restore is checked for tampering, disclosure, duplicate/replayed request identity, unsafe paths and permissions, corruption, resource exhaustion, and failed atomic activation. Structured output to CHANNELS is checked for over-disclosure and semantic authority leakage. Application tenancy, remote auth, and network threats are not applicable only while NG3/NG7 and the local host-account boundary hold.

FEASIBILITY BY GOAL. G1 high confidence for protocol feasibility after A3 produced zero mixed or unreconciled composite results across 2000 forced pre/post cases; production contract conformance remains an implementation gate. G2 high confidence for exact pace states, with A6 and the exact week/time semantics validated by OQ-INSIGHTS-2. G3 low-to-medium confidence: deterministic computation is feasible, but A1 and OQ-INSIGHTS-4/5/10 decide which signals earn v1 scope. G4 medium confidence: immutable local history/recovery patterns are established, while A4 and OQ-INSIGHTS-7 validate value and deletion policy. G5 low-to-medium confidence until A2/OQ-INSIGHTS-6/10 demonstrate useful bounded thresholds with zero false high-severity results. G6 high confidence from the shared Tally structured operation convention. G7 high technical confidence from accepted local privacy/recovery patterns, with host and canary evidence still required before release.

SCOPE AND READINESS. Discovery added C15-C18, NG11, six assumptions, six actor workflows, five external boundaries, six measurable outcomes, and explicit ephemeral-by-default/restatement behavior. OQ-INSIGHTS-1/3/8/9 are resolved. OQ-INSIGHTS-2/6/7 are PRD decisions; A3 and OQ-INSIGHTS-11 now validate the BUDGET-owned public-coherence shape before design. OQ-INSIGHTS-4/5/6 and A1/A2 require evidence only before activating the deferred Forecast, Trend, Anomaly, or Attention scope; OQ-INSIGHTS-10 and A4/A5 require private evaluation before planning the active report. No kill criterion is currently triggered: the bounded public coherence seam passed its protocol spike, no daemon, network, replica, private access, or downstream calculation has been accepted, and persistence remains conditional on security proof. The module is ready for /lex:design INSIGHTS. OQ-INSIGHTS-10 and A4/A5 remain the private evidence gate before planning the active report.

PRD 0.3 — RELEASE REQUIREMENTS. Active v1 scope is one explicit on-demand report over a trusted operation-start As-of Instant, fixed Africa/Johannesburg calendar day/Monday-week/month-to-date windows, coherent public BUDGET/LEDGER evidence, exact window metrics, a transparent exact-rational Linear Pace Baseline, typed evidence-state accounting, deterministic explanations, explicit retained generation, immutable Restatements, metadata-first history/status, dependency-safe deletion, verified backup/restore, private release-policy validation, and the shared non-interactive agent contract. FR-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY through FR-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION define 15 active requirements. Forecast, Trend, Anomaly, and Attention Items are recorded as deferred FRs rather than vague active promises; OQ-INSIGHTS-4/5/6/10 and A1/A2/A4/A5 must provide their named evidence before the affected scope or plan gate is activated; A3 is validated.

PRD TIME AND PACE DECISION. The caller selects a Budget Period but cannot supply a historical clock. INSIGHTS freezes one trusted TimeProvider instant before producer reads and exposes it as the As-of Instant. Effective Date is grouped under Africa/Johannesburg local dates. Day is the As-of calendar date clipped to the Budget Period; week is Monday through Sunday clipped to the period; month-to-date is period start through the earlier of As-of local date or period end. Historical periods use the full month; future periods are Not Started; rolling windows are unsupported. The active pace signal compares month-to-date exact recorded actual with the exact rational planned share elapsedDays/periodDays, gives full-plan overage precedence, handles zero plan without division, exposes current-day and uniform-distribution limitations, and never invents daily or weekly sub-budgets or calls itself a Forecast.

PRD RETENTION DECISION. Generate is always ephemeral. Retain is an explicit owner-attributed idempotent retained-generation operation that calculates and appends one report atomically and accepts no caller-authored report payload. Snapshots remain indefinitely until explicit deletion; there is no automatic expiry. Restatement preserves the predecessor and appends a new linked snapshot for any changed producer identity, policy, schema, or semantic content; an exactly unchanged request returns No Change. Deletion is RESTRICTed while a surviving Restatement references the target, removes one leaf payload atomically, retains a metadata-only Deletion Event, and leaves prior backups unchanged. Restore explicitly reinstates the selected backup's complete point-in-time state.

CONTRACT, AUTHORITY, SEEDS, AND UI PARITY. P1 and the invoking owner host account are the only human/authorization boundary. Read authority covers Discover, Generate, Explain, Get, List, and Status; retained-state operations Retain, Restate, Delete, Backup, and Restore require actor attribution and idempotency. Verify is read-only. INSIGHTS creates no application permissions, role templates, navigation/menu seeds, translation catalogue, or frontend key model. No project sibling currently owns browser mockups or routes. NG2 is the explicit full-stack approval artifact: INSIGHTS owns only channel-neutral structured analytical semantics; a future CHANNELS PRD/design owns pages, browser workflows, navigation, accessibility, translations, notifications, scheduling, and delivery.

PRD TRACEABILITY. G1 maps to FR-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE, FR-INSIGHTS-TRUSTED-ANALYSIS-WINDOWS, FR-INSIGHTS-EXACT-WINDOW-METRICS, and FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-GENERATION through UC-INSIGHTS-001. G2 maps to exact metrics, Linear Pace, Evidence State Accounting, report generation, and explanation through UC-INSIGHTS-001/002. G3 maps to Evidence State Accounting and the deferred Forecast/Trend/Anomaly FRs, gated by FR-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION. G4 maps to Retention, Restatement, History, and Deletion through UC-INSIGHTS-003/004/005. G5 maps to the deferred Attention Item FR and private validation. G6 maps to contract discovery, structured invocation, generation, explanation, and history through UC-INSIGHTS-001/002/003/006. G7 maps to coherent public evidence, structured invocation, retention, history, deletion, recovery, privacy, contract, and offline NFRs. Every FR links P1; 23 planned verification intents cover all 15 active FRs, all seven NFR dimensions, all six workflows, and all six external gates.

PRD READINESS AND KILL RECHECK. No kill criterion is triggered by the active core: it remains one local process, consumes only public contracts, owns no upstream mutation, creates no replica, and makes retained state conditional on privacy/recovery proof. A3 is validated by the disposable protocol spike and DD-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE specifies the coherent producer shape; production conformance must still prove the real BUDGET and LEDGER implementation. Before plan approval, private evidence must establish that the active report improves a named owner decision; failure selects the do-less path. Deferred signal FRs may be activated only after their formula, baseline, sufficiency, limitations, and false-severity evidence are entered into the graph and the PRD is reviewed again.
problem_statement: Surface pain: canonical transactions and exact monthly Budget Positions still leave Dirk manually deciding whether spending is on track, what changed, and which categories deserve attention. 5 Whys: Why are insights needed? Raw facts and plan totals require repeated interpretation. Why is repeated interpretation unreliable? Daily, weekly, and monthly comparisons use unstated windows, formulas, and correction timing. Why does BUDGET not answer this? BUDGET deliberately owns exact plan-versus-actual reconciliation and excludes pace, forecasts, trends, anomalies, recommendations, narratives, and retained reports. Why not ask an agent or spreadsheet ad hoc? The answer would be non-reproducible, could hide sparse evidence or corrections, and could not be compared with what was reported earlier. Root problem: Tally lacks a local, provenance-bound analytics layer that converts exact public BUDGET and LEDGER snapshots into reproducible periodic signals, explicitly distinguishes insufficient evidence, and preserves what the owner was shown without mutating financial truth. Without INSIGHTS, Tally remains a trustworthy record and planning tool but not timely decision support. Journey: the sole Personal Finance Owner, directly or through the AI Agent Host, discovers a versioned INSIGHTS operation, requests an explicit Budget Period, receives an explainable Insight Report bound to the trusted operation-start As-of Instant with exact metrics, a Linear Pace result, and explicit states for deferred or unsupported signal kinds, inspects prior Report Snapshots or a correction-based Restatement, and chooses any action outside INSIGHTS.
why_now: LEDGER, CLASSIFY, and BUDGET now define the canonical facts, category-quality boundary, and exact Budget Position contract. BUDGET explicitly publishes a mutation-free read capability for INSIGHTS, so report semantics should be framed before CHANNELS chooses how to deliver them.

rationale: On-track analysis compares actual spend with monthly category budgets.
Classification quality improves category analytics, while uncategorized spend can still be reported explicitly.
Spend analysis is calculated from canonical transactions.
